title: Directeur Général
exposure_rationale: AI provides advanced data analytics for strategic decision-making and automates some reporting tasks. However, human leadership, complex negotiations, and vision setting remain core to the role.

title: Directeur Financier
exposure_rationale: AI automates financial reporting, forecasting, and risk assessment, enhancing efficiency and accuracy. Strategic financial planning, complex M&A, and stakeholder communication still require human expertise.

title: Directeur des Ressources Humaines
exposure_rationale: AI assists with recruitment screening, HR analytics, personalized learning paths, and administrative tasks. Human empathy, complex employee relations, and cultural development remain critical.

title: Directeur Commercial
exposure_rationale: AI optimizes sales forecasting, lead generation, and CRM analysis, improving efficiency. Building client relationships, closing complex deals, and motivating sales teams still heavily rely on human interaction.

title: Directeur Marketing
exposure_rationale: AI drives personalized campaigns, content generation, market analysis, and ad optimization, transforming marketing operations. Strategic brand development, creative direction, and emotional resonance still require human input.

title: Directeur des Systèmes d'Information
exposure_rationale: AI assists with network monitoring, cybersecurity threat detection, and IT support automation, enhancing system resilience. Strategic IT planning, complex system integrations, and vendor management require human expertise.

title: Chef de Projet
exposure_rationale: AI tools can optimize scheduling, resource allocation, and risk identification, improving project efficiency. Human skills in team leadership, stakeholder communication, and complex problem-solving remain essential.

title: Directeur de Production
exposure_rationale: AI optimizes supply chain, predictive maintenance, and quality control in manufacturing processes. Human oversight is crucial for strategic planning, workforce management, and adapting to unforeseen disruptions.

title: Directeur Logistique
exposure_rationale: AI optimizes route planning, inventory management, and warehouse automation, significantly boosting efficiency. Human decision-making is still needed for complex problem-solving, strategic network design, and supplier negotiations.

title: Directeur d'Agence Bancaire
exposure_rationale: AI automates customer service, loan applications, and fraud detection, reducing the need for physical branches. Human interaction remains important for complex financial advice, relationship building, and managing local teams.

title: Directeur de Magasin
exposure_rationale: AI assists with inventory management, sales forecasting, and personalized customer recommendations, optimizing store operations. Human skills in team leadership, customer service, and visual merchandising are key to creating engaging retail experiences.

title: Directeur d'Hôpital/Clinique
exposure_rationale: AI optimizes resource allocation, patient flow, and administrative tasks, improving healthcare efficiency. Human leadership, ethical decision-making, staff well-being, and community engagement remain central to this role.

title: Directeur de Recherche et Développement
exposure_rationale: AI accelerates data analysis, simulation, and hypothesis generation in research, speeding up discovery. Human creativity, strategic direction, experimental design, and ethical oversight are still paramount for innovation.

title: Directeur Qualité
exposure_rationale: AI enhances defect detection, process optimization, and predictive quality control, improving product standards. Human judgment is needed for root cause analysis, strategic quality improvement, and managing supplier relations.

title: Directeur des Achats
exposure_rationale: AI optimizes supplier selection, contract analysis, and demand forecasting, streamlining procurement processes. Human negotiation skills, strategic sourcing, and risk management in complex supply chains remain crucial.

title: Directeur de la Communication
exposure_rationale: AI assists with content creation, social media monitoring, and audience targeting, enhancing communication reach. Human creativity, crisis management, strategic messaging, and brand reputation building are indispensable.

title: Directeur d'Exploitation
exposure_rationale: AI optimizes resource allocation, process monitoring, and predictive maintenance across operations, boosting efficiency. Human leadership, problem-solving, and strategic operational planning are essential for complex and dynamic environments.

title: Directeur d'Établissement Scolaire
exposure_rationale: AI can assist with administrative tasks, scheduling, and data analysis for student performance, streamlining school management. Human leadership, pedagogical vision, staff development, and community engagement are core to this role.

title: Développeur logiciel
exposure_rationale: Software development is highly exposed to AI coding assistants and automated code generation. While AI augments productivity significantly, architectural decisions still require human expertise.

title: Ingénieur cybersécurité
exposure_rationale: AI assists in threat detection, vulnerability analysis, and automated response. However, complex incident response, strategic planning, and ethical considerations remain human-driven.

title: Analyste de données
exposure_rationale: AI excels at data cleaning, pattern recognition, and predictive modeling, automating many analytical tasks. Human insight is still crucial for interpreting results, formulating hypotheses, and communicating findings.

title: Spécialiste IA/Machine Learning
exposure_rationale: While AI tools assist in model development and optimization, the core work involves designing novel algorithms, understanding complex data, and ethical considerations. AI can augment, but not replace, the creative problem-solving.

title: Chef de projet IT
exposure_rationale: AI can automate scheduling, resource allocation, and risk assessment for projects. However, leadership, stakeholder management, conflict resolution, and strategic decision-making remain inherently human tasks.

title: Architecte cloud
exposure_rationale: AI can optimize cloud resource allocation, monitor performance, and suggest architectural improvements. Yet, designing complex, secure, and scalable cloud solutions requires deep human expertise and strategic vision.

title: Ingénieur DevOps
exposure_rationale: AI can automate CI/CD pipelines, monitor infrastructure, and predict system failures. However, designing robust automation, troubleshooting complex issues, and fostering team collaboration still require human ingenuity.

title: Ingénieur mécanique
exposure_rationale: AI assists in design optimization, predictive maintenance, and simulation. Complex problem-solving, material science innovation, and hands-on prototyping still heavily rely on human expertise and creativity.

title: Ingénieur civil
exposure_rationale: AI can optimize structural design, analyze environmental impacts, and manage construction logistics. However, site-specific challenges, regulatory compliance, and public safety decisions require human judgment.

title: Ingénieur en électronique
exposure_rationale: AI aids in circuit design, simulation, and fault detection in complex systems. Yet, innovative component development, system integration, and hardware-software co-design demand human ingenuity.

title: Chercheur scientifique
exposure_rationale: AI can accelerate data analysis, literature review, and hypothesis generation. However, formulating novel research questions, designing experiments, and interpreting complex results remain human intellectual endeavors.

title: Architecte (Bâtiment)
exposure_rationale: AI can generate design options, optimize energy efficiency, and create realistic renderings. Nevertheless, understanding client vision, aesthetic judgment, and navigating complex urban planning require human creativity and empathy.

title: Ingénieur qualité
exposure_rationale: AI can automate defect detection, analyze process data, and predict quality issues. However, developing new quality standards, root cause analysis, and implementing systemic improvements require human expertise and decision-making.

title: Administrateur systèmes et réseaux
exposure_rationale: AI can automate routine maintenance, monitor system health, and predict outages. While AI handles many operational tasks, complex troubleshooting, security hardening, and strategic infrastructure planning still require human oversight.

title: Consultant ERP
exposure_rationale: AI can assist in data migration, system configuration, and user support. However, understanding complex business processes, customizing solutions, and managing client relationships are highly human-centric tasks.

title: Biologiste
exposure_rationale: AI can analyze genomic data, simulate biological processes, and identify drug candidates. Nevertheless, designing experiments, interpreting complex biological interactions, and ethical considerations require human scientific rigor.

title: Mathématicien/Statisticien
exposure_rationale: AI tools can automate statistical modeling and data visualization. However, developing novel mathematical frameworks, validating complex models, and providing deep theoretical insights remain human intellectual contributions.

title: Ingénieur procédés
exposure_rationale: AI can optimize process parameters, predict equipment failures, and improve efficiency. Yet, designing entirely new processes, troubleshooting complex operational issues, and ensuring safety compliance require human engineering judgment.

title: Technicien support informatique
exposure_rationale: AI can automate routine support tasks and provide intelligent troubleshooting through chatbots and knowledge bases. However, complex issues, human interaction, and on-site interventions still require technician expertise.

title: Développeur web/mobile
exposure_rationale: AI coding assistants and low-code/no-code platforms significantly impact development by generating code and automating repetitive tasks. While AI augments productivity, complex architecture, creative problem-solving, and debugging remain human domains.

title: Technicien maintenance industrielle
exposure_rationale: AI-driven predictive maintenance systems can identify potential failures before they occur, optimizing maintenance schedules. However, the physical repair, diagnosis of complex mechanical issues, and hands-on intervention remain human tasks.

title: Dessinateur-projeteur BTP/Industrie
exposure_rationale: Generative AI and advanced CAD software can automate significant portions of design and drafting, creating initial layouts and optimizing components. While human oversight for complex projects and aesthetic choices is crucial, the demand for manual drafting skills may decrease.

title: Technicien laboratoire Chimie/Biologie
exposure_rationale: AI can automate data analysis, experiment design, and robotic lab processes, increasing efficiency and accuracy. However, interpreting complex results, developing new protocols, handling delicate samples, and ensuring safety require human expertise.

title: Technicien méthodes et qualité
exposure_rationale: AI can optimize production processes, identify quality defects through computer vision, and analyze performance data for continuous improvement. Human technicians are still needed to implement changes, troubleshoot complex issues, and ensure overall quality system integrity and compliance.

title: Technicien automatismes et robotique
exposure_rationale: AI is integral to advanced robotics and automation systems, enhancing their capabilities for tasks like path planning and object recognition. Technicians are crucial for programming, integrating, maintaining, and troubleshooting these complex, AI-driven systems.

title: Technicien en électronique
exposure_rationale: AI can assist in circuit design, fault diagnosis, and automated testing of electronic components, speeding up development and quality control. However, hands-on assembly, precise repair, and innovative problem-solving for new electronic systems require human skills.

title: Technicien cybersécurité
exposure_rationale: AI is heavily used in threat detection, anomaly analysis, and automated response systems to combat sophisticated cyber threats. Technicians are essential for configuring these tools, investigating complex incidents, and developing proactive security strategies that AI cannot fully automate.

title: Technicien géomètre-topographe
exposure_rationale: AI-powered drones and automated mapping software can streamline data collection and initial processing for topographical surveys. However, complex field work, precise site analysis, legal interpretation of boundaries, and client communication still require human expertise.

title: Technicien énergie renouvelable
exposure_rationale: AI can optimize energy production, predict maintenance needs, and manage smart grids for renewable installations. Technicians are vital for the physical installation, hands-on maintenance, troubleshooting, and repair of these complex energy systems.

title: Technicien production industrielle
exposure_rationale: AI-driven process optimization, quality control systems, and robotic automation are increasingly common in industrial production. While AI enhances efficiency, human oversight, problem-solving for unexpected issues, and adaptation to new processes remain critical.

title: Technicien biomédical
exposure_rationale: AI can assist in diagnosing equipment faults, optimizing device performance, and analyzing patient data from medical devices. However, the hands-on maintenance, precise calibration, and complex repair of sophisticated medical equipment require specialized human skills and judgment.

title: Technicien aéronautique
exposure_rationale: AI can aid in predictive maintenance, diagnostics, and flight data analysis for aircraft systems, improving safety and efficiency. However, the rigorous inspection, complex repair, and certification processes for aircraft demand highly skilled human technicians with critical judgment.

title: Technicien CVC
exposure_rationale: AI can optimize energy consumption, predict maintenance needs, and manage smart building climate control systems. However, the physical installation, hands-on repair, and complex troubleshooting of CVC systems, especially in varied environments, are human-intensive tasks.

title: Technicien télécommunications
exposure_rationale: AI can automate network monitoring, fault detection, and service optimization in telecommunications infrastructure. However, infrastructure deployment, complex problem-solving for network outages, and physical maintenance of equipment still require human technicians.

title: Technicien contrôle non destructif
exposure_rationale: AI can enhance image analysis for defect detection in NDT, improving accuracy and speed in identifying flaws. However, setting up equipment, interpreting complex results, adapting to new materials, and certifying findings remain critical human responsibilities requiring specialized knowledge.

title: Secrétaire
exposure_rationale: AI can automate scheduling, email management, document drafting, and information retrieval. While human judgment for complex interactions remains, many routine tasks are susceptible to automation.

title: Assistant administratif
exposure_rationale: Similar to secretaries, administrative assistants perform many tasks that AI can streamline or automate, such as data organization, report generation, and basic communication. Human oversight for critical decisions is still required.

title: Comptable
exposure_rationale: Accounting tasks like data entry, reconciliation, and report generation are highly amenable to AI and automation. AI can process invoices, manage ledgers, and identify anomalies, shifting human roles towards analysis and strategic advice.

title: Aide-comptable
exposure_rationale: Entry-level accounting support roles, heavily focused on data entry and basic processing, are among the most exposed to AI automation. AI can perform these repetitive tasks faster and with fewer errors.

title: Agent d'accueil
exposure_rationale: AI-powered chatbots and virtual assistants can handle routine inquiries, appointment scheduling, and basic information provision. However, complex problem-solving and empathetic human interaction still require a human agent.

title: Conseiller clientèle
exposure_rationale: Customer service is increasingly augmented and automated by AI. Chatbots, voice bots, and AI-driven knowledge bases can resolve many common issues, reducing the need for human agents for routine interactions.

title: Agent de saisie
exposure_rationale: Data entry is one of the most highly exposed occupations to AI. Optical Character Recognition (OCR) and intelligent document processing (IDP) technologies can automate nearly all aspects of data capture and input.

title: Gestionnaire de paie
exposure_rationale: Payroll processing involves complex rules and calculations, which AI can automate significantly. While AI can handle routine calculations and compliance checks, human expertise is still needed for complex cases, legal interpretation, and employee relations.

title: Assistant de direction
exposure_rationale: While AI can assist with scheduling, communication, and document preparation, the strategic and interpersonal aspects of supporting an executive remain human-centric. AI will augment, but not fully replace, these roles.

title: Agent de facturation
exposure_rationale: Billing processes are highly standardized and rule-based, making them very susceptible to AI automation. AI can generate invoices, track payments, and manage accounts receivable with high efficiency.

title: Agent de recouvrement
exposure_rationale: AI can automate initial contact, payment reminders, and even negotiate basic payment plans. However, complex cases requiring empathy, legal understanding, and nuanced negotiation still rely on human agents.

title: Gestionnaire de stock
exposure_rationale: AI can optimize inventory levels, predict demand, and automate ordering processes. While AI improves efficiency, human oversight is crucial for physical inventory management, quality control, and handling unexpected supply chain disruptions.

title: Agent de logistique
exposure_rationale: AI is transforming logistics through route optimization, predictive maintenance, and automated warehouse management. While AI handles data and planning, human agents are essential for coordination, problem-solving, and physical oversight.

title: Agent de production
exposure_rationale: AI can optimize production schedules, monitor quality, and predict equipment failures. While AI automates many aspects of production planning and control, human intervention is still needed for complex problem-solving, quality assurance, and adapting to unforeseen issues.

title: Agent bancaire
exposure_rationale: Many routine banking transactions and inquiries can be handled by AI-powered digital platforms and chatbots. Human agents are increasingly reserved for complex financial advice, sales of specialized products, and resolving intricate customer issues.

title: Agent d'assurance
exposure_rationale: AI can automate claims processing, policy administration, and risk assessment. While AI handles routine tasks, human agents are still vital for personalized advice, complex claims negotiation, and building client relationships.

title: Documentaliste
exposure_rationale: AI can significantly enhance document management, indexing, and information retrieval through natural language processing and semantic search. Human expertise remains crucial for content curation, strategic information architecture, and complex research.

title: Clerc de notaire
exposure_rationale: AI can assist with legal document drafting, contract analysis, and information retrieval for routine cases. However, the nuanced legal interpretation, client counseling, and ethical considerations inherent in notary work require human judgment.

title: Vendeur en magasin
exposure_rationale: AI can assist with inventory management, personalized recommendations, and customer analytics. However, human interaction, empathy, and visual merchandising remain crucial for effective sales and customer experience.

title: Caissier
exposure_rationale: Self-checkout systems and automated payment solutions are increasingly prevalent, directly reducing the need for human cashiers. AI can further optimize these automated processes and handle basic customer queries.

title: Serveur
exposure_rationale: While AI can assist with order taking (e.g., QR codes, tablets) and inventory, the core of a server's role involves direct human interaction, service quality, and problem-solving. These aspects are difficult for AI to replicate.

title: Cuisinier
exposure_rationale: AI can optimize recipes, manage inventory, and even operate some automated cooking stations in industrial settings. However, creativity, sensory evaluation, and complex meal preparation still require human skill and judgment.

title: Aide-soignant
exposure_rationale: This role involves direct physical and emotional care, requiring empathy, communication, and human touch that AI cannot replicate. While AI might assist with scheduling or patient monitoring, the core tasks are inherently human.

title: Agent de sécurité
exposure_rationale: AI-powered surveillance systems, facial recognition, and drone monitoring can augment security operations, automating some detection tasks. However, human judgment, intervention, and de-escalation skills remain essential for complex situations.

title: Coiffeur
exposure_rationale: Hairdressing is a highly manual and creative skill requiring fine motor control, aesthetic judgment, and direct client interaction. AI tools might assist with scheduling or style recommendations, but cannot perform the core service.

title: Esthéticien
exposure_rationale: Similar to hairdressing, this role involves direct physical contact, personalized care, and aesthetic judgment. While AI could analyze skin conditions or recommend products, the application and human touch are irreplaceable.

title: Agent d'entretien
exposure_rationale: Robotics and AI-powered cleaning machines are becoming more sophisticated and widely adopted in commercial and industrial settings. These technologies can automate routine cleaning tasks, reducing the need for human labor in some areas.

title: Agent centre d'appels
exposure_rationale: AI-powered chatbots and voice assistants can handle a significant portion of customer inquiries and support tasks. This automation reduces the need for human agents, especially for routine issues and first-level support.

title: Aide à domicile
exposure_rationale: This role requires a high degree of empathy, trust, and physical assistance for daily living activities, which AI cannot replicate. While AI might assist with scheduling or monitoring, the core tasks are deeply human and personal.

title: Éducateur jeunes enfants
exposure_rationale: Early childhood education relies heavily on human interaction, emotional intelligence, and adapting to individual child development. AI can provide educational tools but cannot replace the nuanced role of a human educator.

title: Pâtissier
exposure_rationale: Pâtisserie is a craft requiring precision, creativity, and sensory evaluation, which are difficult for AI to fully replicate. While AI could optimize recipes or inventory, the artistic and manual aspects remain human-centric.

title: Boulanger
exposure_rationale: Baking involves traditional skills, sensory judgment (e.g., dough consistency, doneness), and often early morning hours. AI might assist with ingredient management or oven control, but the core craft remains human.

title: Barman
exposure_rationale: The role of a barman involves social interaction, mixology skills, and creating a welcoming atmosphere. While AI could manage inventory or suggest recipes, the human element of service and entertainment is paramount.

title: Hôte de caisse
exposure_rationale: Similar to cashiers, this role is highly susceptible to automation through self-checkout and automated payment systems. AI can further streamline these processes and handle basic customer service interactions.

title: Agent de voyages
exposure_rationale: AI-powered travel planning websites and personalized recommendation engines can automate itinerary creation, booking, and price comparison. This significantly reduces the need for human agents for routine travel arrangements.

title: Guide touristique
exposure_rationale: AI can provide detailed historical information, real-time translations, and interactive virtual tours. While human guides offer unique storytelling and personal interaction, AI can replicate much of the informational aspect.

title: Déménageur
exposure_rationale: This is a highly physical job requiring strength, coordination, and problem-solving for logistics and handling fragile items. While AI could optimize routes, the core tasks are manual and not easily automated by current AI.

title: Chauffeur de taxi/VTC
exposure_rationale: The advent of autonomous vehicles, powered by advanced AI, poses a significant long-term threat to this occupation. While full deployment is still some years away, the trend towards self-driving cars is clear.

title: Agent immobilier
exposure_rationale: AI can automate property matching, virtual tours, market analysis, and contract generation. While human negotiation and client relationship building remain important, many intermediary tasks are susceptible to AI automation.

title: Commercial sédentaire
exposure_rationale: AI can automate lead generation, personalized outreach, and initial customer qualification, reducing the need for human agents for routine sales tasks. Chatbots and virtual assistants can handle many inbound inquiries.

title: Fleuriste
exposure_rationale: Floristry involves artistic arrangement, plant care knowledge, and personalized customer service. While AI could assist with inventory or design suggestions, the creative and tactile aspects are inherently human.

title: Gardien d'immeuble
exposure_rationale: Smart building technologies, remote surveillance, and automated access control systems can reduce the need for constant human presence. AI can manage maintenance schedules and monitor building conditions, automating some tasks.

title: Agriculteur polyvalent
exposure_rationale: AI assists with crop monitoring, yield prediction, and resource optimization in general farming. However, the hands-on management of diverse farm operations and adaptation to unforeseen conditions still heavily relies on human judgment and experience.

title: Éleveur d'animaux
exposure_rationale: AI tools can monitor animal health, optimize feeding schedules, and detect behavioral anomalies in livestock. Yet, direct animal care, breeding decisions, and managing complex biological systems require significant human expertise and empathy.

title: Maraîcher producteur légumes
exposure_rationale: AI-powered robots can automate planting, weeding, and harvesting of certain vegetable crops, and AI can optimize irrigation and pest control. However, the nuanced judgment for crop rotation, soil health, and market adaptation remains a human domain.

title: Viticulteur vigneron
exposure_rationale: AI can assist with vineyard monitoring, disease early detection, and even automated pruning in some cases. Nevertheless, the art of winemaking, terroir management, and critical decisions throughout the growing cycle are deeply human-centric.

title: Ouvrier agricole spécialisé
exposure_rationale: While they may operate increasingly automated machinery, many tasks involve manual dexterity, physical labor, and adapting to varied field conditions. AI primarily augments tools rather than replacing the worker's direct involvement in core tasks.

title: Conducteur engins agricoles
exposure_rationale: This role is highly exposed to AI through autonomous driving systems, precision planting, and smart spraying technologies. While human oversight is still crucial, AI increasingly handles the operational execution of complex field tasks.

title: Sylviculteur gestionnaire forestier
exposure_rationale: AI aids in forest inventory, disease detection via satellite imagery, and optimizing timber harvesting plans. However, on-the-ground assessment, ecological understanding, and long-term strategic planning remain complex human tasks.

title: Ouvrier forestier bûcheron
exposure_rationale: While advanced machinery with AI components can automate felling and processing, many tasks still require manual skill, judgment in complex terrain, and adapting to unique tree characteristics. AI primarily enhances equipment rather than replacing the operator.

title: Pêcheur professionnel
exposure_rationale: AI assists with navigation, fish detection, and optimizing routes for fuel efficiency. However, the core tasks of operating fishing gear, adapting to sea conditions, and making real-time decisions based on experience are highly human-dependent.

title: Horticulteur pépiniériste
exposure_rationale: AI can optimize greenhouse environments, detect plant diseases early, and automate some planting or potting tasks. Nevertheless, the aesthetic judgment, plant care expertise, and customer interaction remain crucial human elements.

title: Arboriculteur fruitier
exposure_rationale: AI-powered robots are emerging for fruit harvesting and pruning, and AI can optimize irrigation and pest management. However, the nuanced decisions regarding tree health, variety selection, and market timing still require significant human expertise.

title: Apiculteur professionnel
exposure_rationale: AI can monitor hive health, detect diseases, and track bee activity patterns. Yet, the hands-on care of colonies, understanding bee behavior, and adapting to environmental changes are deeply experiential and human-driven tasks.

title: Maçon
exposure_rationale: Core tasks involve manual skill and physical labor, which are difficult for AI to automate directly. AI could assist with project planning, material optimization, or structural analysis, but not the laying of bricks.

title: Plombier
exposure_rationale: Manual installation and repair require human dexterity and problem-solving in varied environments. AI might optimize routing, diagnose issues via sensors, or assist with complex system design, but not the physical work.

title: Électricien du bâtiment
exposure_rationale: Installation, troubleshooting, and compliance with safety standards demand human judgment and manual skill. AI could aid in circuit design, fault detection, or smart home integration, but not the physical wiring.

title: Menuisier
exposure_rationale: Precision cutting, assembly, and finishing of wood products require fine motor skills and aesthetic judgment. AI can assist with CAD/CAM design, material optimization, and CNC machine programming, but human craftsmanship remains key.

title: Peintre en bâtiment
exposure_rationale: The application of paint and decorative finishes requires adaptability to varied surfaces and aesthetic judgment. While robotic painting exists for industrial settings, it's less practical for diverse residential/commercial sites.

title: Charpentier
exposure_rationale: Constructing and assembling wooden structures demands physical strength, precision, and on-site problem-solving. AI could assist with structural design, material optimization, or prefabrication planning, but not the direct assembly.

title: Couvreur
exposure_rationale: Working at heights, handling heavy materials, and ensuring watertight seals are complex manual tasks. AI might help with drone inspections or material estimates, but the physical installation is human-dependent.

title: Plâtrier-plaquiste
exposure_rationale: Applying plaster or installing drywall requires manual skill, precision, and adaptability to uneven surfaces. While some automation exists for large flat areas, complex geometries and finishing touches remain human tasks.

title: Soudeur
exposure_rationale: While robotic welding is common in mass production, specialized and complex welding (e.g., custom fabrication, repair, exotic materials) still requires highly skilled human welders. AI can optimize weld paths and monitor quality.

title: Mécanicien automobile
exposure_rationale: Diagnostics are increasingly AI-driven, and AI can guide repair procedures. However, the physical disassembly, repair, and reassembly of complex mechanical systems still require human dexterity and problem-solving.

title: Chaudronnier
exposure_rationale: Fabrication of metal structures involves cutting, shaping, and assembling, often with heavy machinery. AI can optimize designs and cutting patterns, but the hands-on fabrication and quality control remain human-centric.

title: Usineur
exposure_rationale: CNC machines are highly automated, and AI can optimize tool paths, material usage, and predictive maintenance. While human oversight and complex setup are still needed, routine operations are increasingly automated.

title: Boulanger-pâtissier
exposure_rationale: The artistry, sensory evaluation, and manual dexterity in baking and pastry making are difficult to replicate. AI might assist with recipe optimization or inventory management, but not the core creative and manual process.

title: Imprimeur
exposure_rationale: Printing processes are highly automated, with AI optimizing color calibration, print quality, and workflow management. Human roles are shifting towards machine supervision, maintenance, and specialized finishing.

title: Bijoutier-joaillier
exposure_rationale: Crafting intricate jewelry requires exceptional manual dexterity, artistic vision, and precision. AI can assist with CAD design, 3D printing of molds, or material sourcing, but the fine handwork remains human.

title: Ébéniste
exposure_rationale: Creating bespoke furniture requires artistic skill, precision, and deep material knowledge. AI can aid in design visualization (CAD), material optimization, or CNC routing, but the craftsmanship and finishing are human.

title: Tailleur/Couturier
exposure_rationale: Custom garment creation involves precise measurements, pattern making, and intricate sewing. AI could assist with pattern generation or fabric optimization, but the fitting, adjustments, and fine hand-sewing are human skills.

title: Jardinier-paysagiste
exposure_rationale: Physical outdoor work, plant knowledge, and aesthetic design are central to this role. AI might assist with plant selection, irrigation optimization, or landscape design, but the manual labor and artistic execution are human.

title: Chauffeur poids lourd
exposure_rationale: AI assists with navigation, route optimization, and advanced driver-assistance systems, enhancing safety and efficiency. However, full autonomous driving for complex logistics and varied road conditions is still a long-term prospect.

title: Conducteur de ligne de production
exposure_rationale: AI optimizes production line efficiency, predicts equipment failures, and automates quality control processes. While human oversight is still crucial for setup and complex troubleshooting, routine tasks are increasingly automated.

title: Cariste
exposure_rationale: Automated Guided Vehicles (AGVs) and AI-powered warehouse robots are rapidly advancing, taking over repetitive material handling and stacking tasks. This significantly reduces the demand for human forklift operators in many settings.

title: Conducteur d'engins de chantier
exposure_rationale: AI assists with precision grading, obstacle detection, and predictive maintenance for heavy machinery. However, the dynamic and complex nature of construction sites still requires human operators for decision-making and adaptability.

title: Opérateur sur machine-outil
exposure_rationale: AI optimizes machining parameters, detects defects through vision systems, and can automate some programming for CNC machines. Yet, human expertise remains essential for complex setups, quality control, and troubleshooting intricate production issues.

title: Assembleur en usine
exposure_rationale: Repetitive assembly tasks are highly susceptible to automation by AI-powered robots, which can perform these actions with greater speed and precision. This trend is leading to significant job displacement in manufacturing.

title: Conducteur de bus/car
exposure_rationale: AI enhances safety features, optimizes routes, and assists with traffic management. However, human drivers are crucial for passenger interaction, navigating complex urban environments, and responding to unforeseen incidents.

title: Opérateur de machine d'impression
exposure_rationale: AI optimizes print quality, predicts maintenance needs, and automates some setup processes for modern printing presses. While human oversight is still required for complex jobs and troubleshooting, automation reduces manual intervention.

title: Conducteur de train
exposure_rationale: AI assists with signaling, speed control, and predictive maintenance for railway systems, enhancing safety and efficiency. However, human drivers are critical for emergency response, passenger management, and navigating complex rail networks.

title: Opérateur de station d'épuration
exposure_rationale: AI monitors water quality, optimizes chemical dosing, and predicts equipment failures in wastewater treatment plants. Human operators are still essential for managing complex systems, performing maintenance, and responding to critical issues.

title: Monteur-assembleur aéronautique
exposure_rationale: AI assists with quality control, defect detection, and optimizing assembly sequences in complex aeronautical manufacturing. However, the high precision, intricate nature, and safety-critical aspects of these tasks demand significant human skill and oversight.

title: Opérateur de four industriel
exposure_rationale: AI optimizes temperature control, energy consumption, and predicts maintenance needs for industrial furnaces, improving efficiency and safety. Human operators are still crucial for managing complex processes, ensuring safety protocols, and troubleshooting.

title: Monteur-câbleur
exposure_rationale: AI-powered robotics are increasingly capable of automating repetitive wiring and cabling tasks, especially in standardized production environments. This reduces the need for manual labor in many industrial settings.

title: Conducteur de machines agricoles
exposure_rationale: AI-driven precision agriculture, autonomous tractors, and smart implements are transforming farming operations, optimizing planting and harvesting. However, human operators are still essential for managing complex field conditions, maintenance, and adapting to unforeseen circumstances.

title: Opérateur de centrale thermique
exposure_rationale: AI optimizes energy production, predicts equipment failures, and manages grid integration in power plants, leading to more automated control rooms. This trend reduces the number of human operators required for routine monitoring and control.

title: Monteur en électronique
exposure_rationale: Automated pick-and-place machines and AI-driven inspection systems are highly efficient in electronics assembly, performing tasks with speed and precision. This significantly reduces the need for manual labor in mass production environments.

title: Agent de propreté
exposure_rationale: Repetitive tasks like floor cleaning can be automated by robotic cleaners and AI-driven scheduling. However, complex spaces, detailed sanitation, and human interaction still require human workers, limiting full automation.

title: Aide de cuisine
exposure_rationale: AI can assist with inventory management, recipe optimization, and basic food preparation tasks like cutting. However, the core role involves manual dexterity, hygiene, and adapting to varied kitchen environments, which are less susceptible to full automation.

title: Manœuvre du bâtiment
exposure_rationale: Repetitive heavy lifting, demolition, and basic site preparation tasks can be augmented or replaced by robotic equipment and automated machinery. However, adapting to varied site conditions, problem-solving, and coordination with skilled trades still require human judgment.

title: Ouvrier agricole
exposure_rationale: Agriculture is seeing significant automation with robotic harvesters, drones for monitoring, and AI-driven irrigation and pest control. Many manual tasks like picking, planting, and sorting are highly susceptible to replacement by specialized machinery.

title: Manutentionnaire
exposure_rationale: Warehouse automation, including autonomous guided vehicles (AGVs), robotic arms for picking, and automated sorting systems, directly impacts material handling. While human oversight and handling of complex items remain, many repetitive tasks are being automated.

title: Agent de collecte des déchets
exposure_rationale: While the core task of collecting waste is physical, AI can optimize routes and predict waste volumes for efficiency. Robotic arms for bin lifting exist but are not widespread, and human judgment is still needed for varied collection scenarios.

title: Vendeur non spécialisé
exposure_rationale: AI-powered e-commerce, self-checkout kiosks, and automated inventory management reduce the need for basic sales roles. Human interaction for complex sales or personalized advice remains, but elementary sales tasks are vulnerable to automation.

title: Employé de libre-service
exposure_rationale: AI-powered inventory management systems and robotic shelf-stockers are becoming more common in large retail environments. These technologies can significantly reduce the need for human shelf fillers, especially for repetitive stocking tasks.

title: Agent de service hospitalier
exposure_rationale: While some cleaning and logistics tasks can be automated, the role involves direct interaction with patients, maintaining hygiene in sensitive environments, and assisting staff. AI's impact is more on optimizing workflows and scheduling than direct task replacement.

title: Ouvrier d'assemblage
exposure_rationale: Manufacturing assembly lines are highly susceptible to automation by industrial robots and AI-driven systems. Repetitive, precise tasks are ideal for robotic replacement, leading to significant job displacement in this sector.

title: Ouvrier de manutention
exposure_rationale: General manual labor, especially involving repetitive lifting, carrying, and sorting, is increasingly being automated by robotics and specialized machinery. AI can optimize workflows and direct robotic systems, reducing the need for human intervention.

title: Nettoyeur de véhicules
exposure_rationale: Automated car wash systems already handle much of the basic exterior cleaning. AI could further optimize these systems, but detailed interior cleaning, specialized vehicle care, and customer interaction still require human input.

title: Laveur de vitres
exposure_rationale: Robotic window cleaning systems are being developed and deployed for large, high-rise buildings, reducing the need for human cleaners in these contexts. However, smaller buildings and complex architectural designs still require manual work.

title: Ouvrier forestier
exposure_rationale: Forestry operations are increasingly mechanized with felling machines and automated processing. AI can optimize harvesting plans and monitor forest health, further reducing the need for manual labor in many tasks.

title: Employé de restauration rapide
exposure_rationale: Fast food kitchens are increasingly adopting automation for tasks like frying, burger assembly, and drink dispensing. AI can optimize order flow and ingredient management, potentially reducing the need for human staff in repetitive preparation roles.

title: Officier de l'armée de Terre
exposure_rationale: Strategic planning and command can be AI-augmented, especially for intelligence analysis and logistics optimization. However, leadership, human judgment, and ethical decisions in combat remain central to the role.

title: Sous-officier de la Gendarmerie
exposure_rationale: Law enforcement and public order roles are highly human-centric, requiring direct interaction, empathy, and nuanced judgment. AI might assist with data analysis for crime prediction or administrative tasks, but not core duties.

title: Militaire du rang
exposure_rationale: This role primarily involves physical tasks, direct combat, and human teamwork in often unpredictable environments. AI's role is mostly in supporting systems like drones or robotics, rather than replacing the individual soldier.

title: Pilote de chasse
exposure_rationale: While flying requires immense skill, AI can significantly assist with navigation, target acquisition, sensor fusion, and even autonomous flight modes. Human decision-making, especially in complex combat scenarios, remains crucial.

title: Technicien maintenance aéronautique militaire
exposure_rationale: AI can greatly assist in predictive maintenance, diagnostics, and optimizing repair schedules for complex aircraft systems. However, hands-on repair, critical problem-solving, and adaptation to unforeseen issues still require human expertise.

title: Médecin militaire
exposure_rationale: AI can aid in diagnostics, medical imaging analysis, and administrative tasks, improving efficiency. However, patient care, surgical procedures, and empathetic communication in high-stress military environments are inherently human roles.

title: Spécialiste cyberdéfense militaire
exposure_rationale: This role is highly exposed to AI, as AI is used both by attackers and defenders for threat detection, anomaly analysis, and automated response. Human expertise is essential for strategic defense, complex incident response, and adapting to evolving threats.

title: Médecin généraliste
exposure_rationale: AI can assist with diagnostics and administrative tasks, but the core of general practice involves complex human interaction, empathy, and nuanced decision-making that is difficult to automate. The direct patient relationship is paramount.

title: Médecin spécialiste (Chirurgien)
exposure_rationale: While AI and robotics can assist in surgical planning and execution, the critical decision-making, manual dexterity, and adaptability in unforeseen circumstances remain human. AI's role is primarily assistive, not substitutive.

title: Infirmier diplômé d'État
exposure_rationale: Nursing involves extensive direct patient interaction, emotional support, and complex physical care. AI can streamline administrative tasks and monitor vital signs, but cannot replace the human element of care and empathy.

title: Professeur des écoles
exposure_rationale: AI can personalize learning paths and generate educational content, but the nuanced aspects of classroom management, fostering social-emotional development, and adapting to diverse student needs require human intelligence and empathy.

title: Professeur de collège/lycée
exposure_rationale: AI can assist with lesson planning, content creation, and some grading. However, effective pedagogy, inspiring critical thinking, managing classroom dynamics, and providing individualized mentorship remain human-centric.

title: Kinésithérapeute
exposure_rationale: Physiotherapy relies heavily on hands-on treatment, personalized exercise guidance, and understanding patient pain and progress through direct interaction. AI can track progress or suggest exercises but cannot perform the physical therapy itself.

title: Pharmacien d'officine
exposure_rationale: AI can automate prescription verification, drug interaction checks, and inventory management. However, patient counseling, complex medication reviews, and ethical decision-making require human expertise and judgment.

title: Chirurgien-dentiste
exposure_rationale: AI can enhance diagnostic imaging and assist with treatment planning. Nevertheless, the precise manual dexterity required for procedures, managing patient anxiety, and adapting to individual oral anatomies are inherently human skills.

title: Sage-femme
exposure_rationale: Midwifery is profoundly human-centric, involving intimate physical assistance, emotional support, and critical decision-making during childbirth. AI can monitor vital signs but cannot replicate the empathetic and hands-on care.

title: Psychologue clinicien
exposure_rationale: While AI can assist with diagnostic assessments and data analysis, the core of clinical psychology lies in building a therapeutic relationship, understanding complex human emotions, and providing empathetic, nuanced intervention.

title: Orthophoniste
exposure_rationale: Speech therapy requires direct, personalized interaction to assess and treat communication disorders. AI can provide diagnostic support or generate exercises, but the human therapist's ability to adapt, motivate, and empathize is crucial.

title: Enseignant-chercheur (Université)
exposure_rationale: AI can significantly aid in literature review, data analysis, and content generation for teaching and research. However, formulating novel research questions, critical evaluation, and mentoring students require advanced human cognitive abilities.

title: Assistant de service social
exposure_rationale: Social work is fundamentally about human connection, empathy, and advocacy for vulnerable individuals. AI can streamline administrative tasks and information gathering, but cannot replace the human element of support and intervention.

title: Éducateur spécialisé
exposure_rationale: This role involves intensive, personalized support for individuals with disabilities or social difficulties. Building trust, providing emotional support, and adapting interventions in real-time are deeply human and resistant to automation.

title: Diététicien
exposure_rationale: AI can generate personalized meal plans and analyze dietary data. However, motivating behavioral change, understanding individual preferences and cultural contexts, and providing empathetic counseling require human interaction.

title: Ergothérapeute
exposure_rationale: Occupational therapy involves hands-on assessment and intervention to help individuals regain function. While AI can assist with adaptive technologies or assessment tools, the direct, personalized, and empathetic therapeutic process is human.

title: Cadre de santé
exposure_rationale: AI can optimize scheduling, resource allocation, and analyze performance data. However, leading and motivating a team, resolving interpersonal conflicts, and making complex ethical decisions in healthcare management require human leadership skills.

title: Psychologue de l'Éducation Nationale
exposure_rationale: AI can provide career path suggestions and analyze student profiles. Yet, offering personalized guidance, understanding individual aspirations and challenges, and providing empathetic support during critical life choices are human-centric.

title: Consultant en stratégie
exposure_rationale: AI can assist with data analysis, market research, and report generation, augmenting consultant productivity. However, strategic thinking, client relationship management, and complex problem-solving remain human-centric.

title: Analyste financier
exposure_rationale: AI excels at processing vast financial data, identifying patterns, and automating routine analysis tasks. While AI supports decision-making, human judgment is crucial for interpreting complex market dynamics and ethical considerations.

title: Responsable marketing digital
exposure_rationale: AI is heavily used in digital marketing for audience targeting, content generation, campaign optimization, and performance analytics. Human creativity, strategic oversight, and brand storytelling are still essential for effective campaigns.

title: Responsable des ressources humaines
exposure_rationale: AI can automate recruitment screening, payroll processing, and provide data insights for HR decisions. However, human empathy, conflict resolution, talent development, and strategic workforce planning require human interaction and judgment.

title: Chef de projet informatique
exposure_rationale: AI tools can assist with project planning, risk assessment, and task management, improving efficiency. Nevertheless, leadership, team coordination, stakeholder communication, and problem-solving in complex technical environments remain human responsibilities.

title: Data Scientist
exposure_rationale: Data science heavily relies on AI/ML models for analysis, prediction, and pattern recognition. While AI automates many tasks, human expertise is crucial for problem formulation, model interpretation, ethical considerations, and communicating insights.

title: Avocat d'affaires
exposure_rationale: AI can assist with legal research, document review, and contract analysis, speeding up routine tasks. However, complex legal reasoning, negotiation, client advocacy, and courtroom representation require human judgment and interpersonal skills.

title: Juriste d'entreprise
exposure_rationale: AI tools can streamline contract drafting, compliance checks, and legal document management. Yet, understanding nuanced legal implications, advising on strategic decisions, and managing corporate governance require human expertise and contextual understanding.

title: Travailleur social spécialisé
exposure_rationale: AI has minimal direct impact on the core functions of social work, which rely on empathy, direct human interaction, and complex emotional support. While AI might assist with administrative tasks, the human relationship is paramount.

title: Professeur agrégé du secondaire
exposure_rationale: While AI can assist with lesson planning, grading, and providing personalized learning resources, the core of teaching involves human interaction, classroom management, inspiring students, and adapting to diverse learning needs. These aspects are difficult for AI to replicate.

title: Professeur des universités
exposure_rationale: AI can aid in research, data analysis, and content generation for lectures. However, the critical thinking, original research, mentorship, and complex pedagogical strategies involved in higher education remain human-led.

title: Journaliste d'investigation
exposure_rationale: AI can assist with data mining, fact-checking, and generating initial drafts of articles. However, the ethical judgment, source development, critical analysis, and narrative construction essential for investigative journalism are uniquely human.

title: Chargé de communication institutionnelle
exposure_rationale: AI can generate content, analyze public sentiment, and optimize communication channels. Nevertheless, crafting strategic messages, managing crises, building relationships with stakeholders, and ensuring brand consistency require human insight and judgment.

title: Architecte logiciel
exposure_rationale: AI tools can assist with code generation, design pattern suggestions, and system optimization. However, defining complex system architectures, making strategic technology choices, and ensuring scalability and security require deep human expertise and leadership.

title: Manipulateur en Électroradiologie Médicale
exposure_rationale: AI can enhance image interpretation and automate some equipment operations. However, patient interaction, precise positioning, and critical judgment in complex cases remain human-dependent.

title: Technicien de laboratoire médical
exposure_rationale: Many lab processes are already automated, and AI can further enhance analysis and diagnostics. Human oversight, quality control, and complex problem-solving are still essential.

title: Préparateur en pharmacie
exposure_rationale: AI can optimize inventory management, verify prescriptions, and automate dispensing. However, patient counseling, complex compounding, and ethical judgment require human expertise.

title: Diététicien nutritionniste
exposure_rationale: AI can generate personalized meal plans and analyze dietary data. Yet, understanding individual needs, motivational coaching, and adapting to complex health conditions require human interaction.

title: Éducateur de jeunes enfants
exposure_rationale: Involves direct care, emotional support, and fostering the development of young children. These tasks are inherently human-centric and require empathy and adaptability that AI lacks.

title: Moniteur éducateur
exposure_rationale: Focuses on daily support and social integration for individuals with difficulties. The core of the work is human relationship building and adapting to individual needs, which AI cannot perform.

title: Conseiller en insertion professionnelle
exposure_rationale: AI can assist with job matching and resume optimization. However, personalized coaching, motivational support, and understanding individual barriers to employment require human interaction.

title: Animateur socio-culturel
exposure_rationale: Involves facilitating group activities, fostering social interaction, and adapting to diverse audiences. These tasks are inherently human-centric, relying on creativity and interpersonal skills.

title: Comptable général
exposure_rationale: Many routine accounting tasks like data entry, reconciliation, and basic report generation are highly susceptible to automation. Human roles will shift towards analysis and strategic advice.

title: Chargé de clientèle bancaire
exposure_rationale: Routine customer inquiries and simple transactions are increasingly handled by AI and digital platforms. Human roles will focus on complex financial advice and relationship management.

title: Chargé de ressources humaines
exposure_rationale: AI can streamline recruitment, analyze HR data, and automate administrative processes. However, employee relations, strategic planning, and complex decision-making remain human-centric.

title: Assistant juridique / Paralégal
exposure_rationale: AI can significantly automate legal research, document review, and contract analysis. Human roles will shift towards complex case strategy, client interaction, and ethical judgment.

title: Technicien gestion de stock
exposure_rationale: AI can optimize inventory levels, automate warehouse operations, and improve logistics planning. Human roles will focus on system oversight, problem-solving, and strategic supply chain management.

title: Assistant marketing digital
exposure_rationale: AI can assist with content creation, audience targeting, and campaign performance analysis. Human creativity, strategic planning, and understanding market nuances remain crucial.

title: Technicien en assurances
exposure_rationale: AI can automate claims processing, assess risks more accurately, and personalize insurance products. Human roles will focus on complex cases, client relationships, and ethical considerations.

title: Gestionnaire administratif et financier
exposure_rationale: Many administrative and financial tasks, such as data entry, report generation, and budget tracking, are highly susceptible to AI automation. Human roles will shift to analysis and strategic oversight.
